raft.name: RAFT
raft.longName: Rainfall-runoff Analysis Modelling and Forecasting Tools
raft.tagline: We work across rainfall and runoff in cities and riverine areas — so communities can raft through hydroclimatological extremes.
raft.taglineVerbatim: We are working on different aspects of rainfall and runoff in both cities and riverine areas so that we raft through hydroclimatological extremes.
raft.shortPitch: A hydroinformatics research group and innovation lab at IIT Hyderabad, building data-, physics- and AI-driven systems for rainfall-runoff analysis, flood forecasting and climate-resilient decision-making.
raft.whatWeDoVerbatim: Collection, analysis, modelling, and development and dissemination of products in the context of rainfall and runoff — using physics-, physically- and AI/ML-based techniques.
raft.vision: To develop a flood information system for both cities and riverine areas so that scientists, engineers, policymakers and citizens can talk to each other — and find realistic solutions that make cities and villages flood resilient.
raft.mission: To collect, analyse and model rainfall and runoff across urban and riverine landscapes, and to translate that science into trusted, accessible products that strengthen flood resilience for communities, agencies and decision-makers.

origin.note: Origin year provided by the RAFT team; not independently verifiable from public sources.

ufis.focus: Developed with a focus on Hyderabad to minimise the effects of urban floods, integrating meteorological, hydrological and stakeholder-relevant aspects.
ufis.goalVerbatim: Provide reliable and accurate rain and flood forecasts, then deliver flood-relevant information in advance at different locations within the city.
howBuilt: Modelling to simulate and forecast rainfall amounts, flood depths and extent
Development of products as per the needs of policymakers and stakeholders
ufis.maturity: Reporting tools (SnapFlood) are deployed; the predictive and alerting components of UFIS are in active development.

approach.title: Collect
approach.description: Flood-relevant data from crowdsourcing (SnapFlood), satellite rainfall, radar reflectivity and social-media signals.

approach.title: Analyse
approach.description: Make sense of hydroclimatological data across space and time scales to understand how floods form and evolve.

approach.title: Model & Forecast
approach.description: Simulate and forecast rainfall amounts, flood depths and extent using physics-, physically- and AI/ML-based methods.

approach.title: Disseminate
approach.description: Build and deliver products tailored to the needs of policymakers, agencies, engineers and citizens.

values.title: Scientific rigour
values.description: Peer-reviewed methods, validated models and honest treatment of uncertainty.

values.title: Real-world impact
values.description: Research measured by the decisions it improves and the communities it protects.

values.title: Open collaboration
values.description: Working across institutions, agencies and disciplines on shared water challenges.

values.title: Climate resilience
values.description: Tools that help cities and villages adapt to a more extreme, less predictable climate.

timeline.title: PhD, University of Colorado Boulder
timeline.description: Dr. Satish Kumar Regonda completes doctoral research in hydroclimatology.

timeline.title: Hydroclimatology comes to IITH
timeline.description: Dr. Regonda joins the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Hyderabad.

timeline.title: RAFT initiative takes shape
timeline.description: The RAFT research direction forms; Dr. Regonda heads the IITH Department of Climate Change (2019–2022).

timeline.title: Riverine flood modelling
timeline.description: 2D flood-inundation modelling in the Godavari basin; urban flood-hotspot work presented at AGU.

timeline.title: Mapping Hyderabad’s rainfall
timeline.description: High-temporal-resolution rainfall climatology over Hyderabad published in Urban Climate.

timeline.title: SnapFlood goes live
timeline.description: SnapFlood deployed on Web, Android and iOS; the VarshaMitra mascot launches to promote community flood reporting.

timeline.title: AI for sustainable cities
timeline.description: RAFT’s work connects to the Ministry of Education’s AI Centre of Excellence for Sustainable Cities.

timeline.title: Scaling UFIS
timeline.description: Advancing the Urban Flood Information System toward real-time forecasting, alerting and advisory.
